Package org.apache.juneau.commons.function

Interface BeanFactory<T>

Type Parameters:
T - The type of bean this factory creates.
All Known Implementing Classes:
BeanFactory.Void
Functional Interface:
This is a functional interface and can therefore be used as the assignment target for a lambda expression or method reference.
@FunctionalInterface public interface BeanFactory<T>
A universal factory interface used with @Marshalled(factory=X.class) for any bean type the framework needs to instantiate.

This interface provides a single DI hook for all framework-managed classes including BeanConsumer, BeanSupplier, BeanChannel, ObjectSwap subclasses, and any ordinary bean class parsed from JSON/XML/etc.

When a class annotated with @Marshalled(factory=MyFactory.class) needs to be instantiated, the framework resolves the factory as follows:

  1. Look up the factory class in the BeanStore (e.g. Spring ApplicationContext)
  2. If not found in the store, attempt to instantiate the factory directly via no-arg constructor or getInstance() static method
  3. If both fail, throw IllegalArgumentException
Example (Spring integration):

// Spring singleton factory that creates per-request ItemChannel instances @Component public class ItemChannelFactory implements BeanFactory<ItemChannel> { @Autowired DataSource ds; @Override public ItemChannel create() { return new ItemChannel(ds); } } // The target class declares which factory creates it @Marshalled(factory=ItemChannelFactory.class) public class ItemChannel implements BeanChannel<Item> { ... }

See Also:
  • BeanConsumer - Parse-only lifecycle interface
  • BeanSupplier - Serialize-only lifecycle interface
  • BeanChannel - Round-trip lifecycle interface
  • Marshalling

    static final class 
    BeanFactory.Void
    Sentinel class used as the default value for @BeanType(factory=...) and @BeanProp(factory=...) when no factory is specified.
